anOfferYouCantRefuse.title: Interlude I: An Offer You Can’t Refuse
anOfferYouCantRefuse.interlude1:
“What you have recovered is no mere curio,” Randall smirks as you admire the ancient artifact—a vaguely humanoid figure with a heavy, octopoid head and squid-like features. “Through much painstaking research, I have pinpointed the relative coordinates of an ancient city: a stretch of ocean in the South Pacific where many sailing vessels have disappeared with no explanation. I need someone bold and resourceful to lead an expedition to the ancient city. I need that person to be you. Should you accept, I shall absolve you of any remaining debt and provide you enough money to retire comfortably. Very comfortably.”

Your mind wanders back to those other “concerns.”
anOfferYouCantRefuse.chooseTask: In player order, each investigator must choose a different Task and read its text.

walkInFaith.title: Task: Walk in Faith
walkInFaith.body: All your life, you have believed in something greater than yourself, something that guides your hand and directs your path. Months ago, you recognized the unblinking eye set in the stained glass of Tillinghast’s shop from your dreams. It was a sign. You can’t help but feel that this is the reason you were led here. You have no choice but to say yes.

toeTheLine.title: Task: Toe the Line
toeTheLine.body: You were a bright star, rising through the ranks—until your sudden and ignominious fall from grace. Your former colleague saw to it that you’d never come by honest work, so you turned to Tillinghast to make a living. You’ve always hoped he might help you get your old position back and restore your good name.

noPlaceLikeHome.title: Task: No Place Like Home
noPlaceLikeHome.body: Mr. Tillinghast has always been good to you. When you were down on your luck, it was Tillinghast who got you off the streets, who paid for a warm bed and a hot meal at Ma’s Boarding House. The money he’s promised for this job will finally get you back on your feet. You know he’s good for it.

goodMoney.title: Task: Good Money
goodMoney.body: Over the years, you’ve had to do plenty of things you’d never admit in polite society. But that’s all over now. This is your chance for the fresh start Tillinghast promised you years ago when he paid off your old debt. With what Tillinghast is promising, you should finally be able to live life on your own terms—so long as you survive one more cockamamie errand.

doNoHarm.title: Task: Do No Harm
doNoHarm.body: People have always depended on you when things go wrong. Randall has seen your talents put to good use over the course of your work with him; the slippery businessman has never given you easy work, but you’ve always risen to the challenge. If you nail this last job, he’s promised you enough to live your dreams.

proveYourWorth.title: Task: Prove Your Worth
proveYourWorth.body: You’ve heard them talking about you behind your back. “Dead weight,” they called you. You’ll show them. Randall has promised you everything you need to rise to the top, to show those catty bastards what you’re really made of. You need this job to prove to everyone that you’re not the failure your peers say you are.

dreamsOfDestruction.title: Task: Dreams of Destruction
dreamsOfDestruction.body: Every night for the past year, you have been wracked with awful nightmares. Visions of a terrible calamity. Streets in ruin. Spectral forms of the drowned. And above it all some nameless, looming form. You entered Tillinghast’s shop hoping to find answers in his occult tomes, and soon found yourself ensnared in his growing enterprise. Randall has promised you answers.

plumbTheDepths.title: Task: Plumb the Depths
plumbTheDepths.body: You never meant to get involved with Randall, or the mob, or any of this. But you can never suppress the inexorable itch to understand the world around you. Although you have been warned time and again that your curiosity would be the end of you, you can’t deny the allure of the unknown. This job will make your career, if you can just play your cards right.

anOfferYouCantRefuse.interlude2: You narrow your eyes at Randall. There’s something he’s not telling you. Some catch. There’s always a catch with Tillinghast. You ask him what it is and he sighs. “I thought it was obvious, but this expedition is likely to be rather perilous. Which is why I thought to entrust it to you. You’ve proven yourself to be quite resourceful. And as I said, I intend to reward you handsomely.”
anOfferYouCantRefuse.interlude3: There has to be a catch. Tillinghast wouldn’t offer such tempting bait otherwise. You’re through with him. You shake your head no. “Then get out,” Tillinghast says icily. “I have no further use for you. Rest assured, however, that if you speak a word of this to anyone, you will regret it.” Without a word, you walk out of the shop, leaving behind whatever wonders—and dangers—Tillinghast had in store for you. The investigators lose the campaign.
anOfferYouCantRefuse.interlude4: Whatever his reasons for burying the lede, the offer is too tempting to refuse. Ruby and Andy nod along with you in agreement. Tillinghast’s face breaks into a familiar smirk. “How lovely. I’d hoped you would say yes. Truly, this is the opportunity of a lifetime. I assure you this will change everything.”

expeditionToRlyeh.title: Interlude II: Expedition to R’lyeh
expeditionToRlyeh.body: It is early evening when you first see the ancient city—a tangle of collapsed towers, labyrinthine boroughs, and jagged paths that loop in on themselves like a rat’s nest. In the morning, you discuss which route to take. Andy wants to follow the coastline to the ruined western wall near the mouth of the cove. Ruby, perennially comfortable with heights, wants to take to the skyline to map the city, pointing at a distant, cathedral-like structure further inland. Depending on which character you want to follow, you will either explore the ancient city from west to east, or east to west. You must decide, as a group.
expeditionToRlyeh.west: Follow Andy along the shoreline to the Western Wall.
expeditionToRlyeh.east: Accompany Ruby through the Obsidian Canyons toward a massive inland structure.
expeditionToRlyeh.westernExpedition: Something about the distant, ruined wall and submerged spires calls to you. After agreeing to accompany Andy westward, he sighs with relief. “I don’t want to worry you, but I could swear I saw something human-shaped swimming below. I’ll be glad for the company.” Before parting ways, he passes you the obsidian tablet he found, stammering that he isn’t much of a linguist.
expeditionToRlyeh.easternExpedition: Since you landed, you haven’t been able to take your eyes off the phantasmagoric skyline. After agreeing to accompany her eastward, you catch the faintest hint of a smile from Ruby. “Fine, you can come along. As long as you don’t hold me back,” she smirks. A fell wind whistles through the perilous canyons, smelling of ancient decay and another, sweeter smell. Storm clouds churn in the distance threateningly.
